focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jill seeks help for a wrongful arrest but is redirected between departments; her unstamped receipt prevents progress, and a surveillance robot crashes after she swats it away.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: displays signs of career advancement while exhibiting nervous overstatement about his personal and professional life
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reinforcing his image of success and offering unsolicited career advice to Sam
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: comes across as outwardly confident but shows signs of defensiveness and possible stress
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: discrepancy between his enthusiastic claims of success and his anxious delivery
evidence_quotes: JACK Sam! SAM Jack!
SAM You sound worried. JACK Me? - if I'm worried about anyone, it's you.

relation_updates: reinforces professional distance from Sam
asserts superiority in career trajectory
status_updates: positioned as trusted aide to Mr. Helpmann
auxiliary.persona_anchor: career-driven bureaucrat with fragile self-assurance

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam awkwardly navigates a tense conversation about Alison's ears until Helpmann arrives, mistaking her name, and privately requests Sam's help, with Jack covering the error.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jack allows Mr. Helpmann to misname his wife and subtly signals her to accept the error without correction, maintaining outward composure
timeline_nodes.goal_state: preserving hierarchy and social decorum in front of superiors
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mr. Helpmann's mistake is unchallenged; the interaction proceeds as if the name Barbara were correct
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: pressure to suppress personal identity to accommodate authority figures
evidence_quotes: Jack quickly jumps in, correcting nothing and playing along with the error, urging his wife to do the same.

relation_updates: reinforces deference to Mr. Helpmann
manages public perception of his wife
auxiliary.persona_anchor: loyal enforcer of bureaucratic decorum

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: SAM convinces JACK to remove Layton from the detain list despite protocol, borrows her dossier to reach her before security, and receives an executive package as JACK expresses trust and holiday goodwill.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: grants Sam access to confidential files on Jill Layton despite protocol, facilitating Sam's off-the-record investigation
timeline_nodes.goal_state: balancing institutional duty with personal loyalty to Sam
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: becomes complicit in Sam's unauthorized inquiry by providing restricted information
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: conflict between personal allegiance and adherence to bureaucratic procedure
evidence_quotes: JACK For my sins.
OK. Come back this afternoon, about four o'clock. If you give me the number of the case, I'll have the dossier here waiting.
You clever bastard! I might have guessed. You only moved in today and you're already hot on the bloody trail.

relation_updates: shifts toward collaboration on a sensitive case
acknowledges shared risk in subverting procedure
status_updates: in control of Security Level Three dossier access
positioned as key enabler in Sam's investigation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: reluctant ally leveraging institutional power

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: SAM convinces JACK to remove Layton from the detain list despite protocol, borrows her dossier to reach her before security, and receives an executive package as JACK expresses trust and holiday goodwill.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: overrides protocol to assist Sam by removing Layton from the detention list and lending her dossier, acting on personal trust
timeline_nodes.goal_state: contain the Layton situation discreetly without departmental exposure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: complicit in an unauthorized effort to intercept Layton ahead of security forces
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: risk of exposure for bypassing official procedures
evidence_quotes: JACK Get her out of circulation - I've put her on the detention list.
JACK Of course. We went to school together. You're my oldest friend.
JACK You're the only person I can trust.
JACK Er ... alright. For Christ's sake don't lose it. Here, you'd better sign for it.

relation_updates: reinforces deep personal trust with Sam Lowry
maintains loyalty based on shared history
auxiliary.persona_anchor: institutional insider using privilege for personal loyalty

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jack warns Sam of escalating danger after Tuttle's attack on Sam's block, confirms he believes Tuttle and the girl are allied, and leaves urgently, leaving Sam with unresolved tension.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: warns Sam of danger after Tuttle's attack on his residential block, confirms belief in Tuttle and the girl's alliance, and departs abruptly in tactical gear
timeline_nodes.goal_state: warn Sam of danger while maintaining professional distance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: prioritizes institutional response by leaving for active duty, emotionally disengaging from Sam
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension between duty and personal loyalty
evidence_quotes: He is putting on his executive-styled bullet-proof vest and packing his "Secret Connection" briefcase.
SAM is dishevelled and causing acute sartorial embarrassment to JACK, who is trying to distance himself from him.
JACK responds that the case has become much more complicated since their last conversation.

relation_updates: shifts from personal ally to formal distance
rejects Sam's emotional appeal
status_updates: responds to active sabotage in Sam's residential block
auxiliary.persona_anchor: bureaucrat aligning with system under pressure

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jack believes the Buttle/Tuttle confusion was deliberately planned, emphasizing interconnected causes and effects, while Sam considers coincidence possible.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: insists the Buttle/Tuttle confusion was deliberately orchestrated from within, rejecting accidental explanation
timeline_nodes.goal_state: convince Sam that the error was intentional and part of a larger system of control
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: maintains that the bureaucratic error was a planned internal act, not random
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: who inside was responsible and what their purpose was
evidence_quotes: (whispers) This whole Buttle/Tuttle confusion was obviously planned from the inside. Bye bye.

relation_updates: disagrees with Sam's interpretation of events
status_updates: adopts conspiratorial view of bureaucratic errors
auxiliary.persona_anchor: believes in an ordered, interconnected system where nothing is accidental

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jack and Sam exit the lift; Sam insists someone is innocent, but Jack, feeling strained, asks Sam to stay away until the situation resolves.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: acknowledges bond with Sam but explicitly requests distance during the crisis
timeline_nodes.goal_state: avoid entanglement in Sam's situation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: strained but acknowledged personal connection persists
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: conflict between personal loyalty and institutional pressure
evidence_quotes: JACK Sam - we've always been close, haven't we?
SAM (eagerly) Yes we have, Jack!
JACK Well, could you stay away from me until this thing blows over.

relation_updates: requests emotional and physical distance from Sam
auxiliary.persona_anchor: friendship strained by systemic demands

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam pleads with Jack, who is emotionally unstable, before Tuttle and armed commandos burst in, kill Jack and guards, detonate an explosion, and free Sam.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: interrogator conducting information retrieval on Sam
timeline_nodes.salient_development: reveals identity to Sam, expresses personal betrayal and emotional distress, then attempts to resume professional duty before being killed
timeline_nodes.goal_state: conduct interrogation while maintaining emotional detachment
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: killed while attempting to perform interrogation, emotional conflict with Sam unresolved
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: mutual fear and misunderstanding between Jack and Sam, with no resolution before Jack's death
evidence_quotes: He lifts up his mask to reveal sweaty face, contorted with fear and anger.
JACK How could you do this to me?
JACK (pulling down mask) Shut up! This is a professional relationship!
JACK is cut down.

relation_updates: expresses rage and sense of personal betrayal toward Sam
reverts to professional role under pressure
auxiliary.persona_anchor: duty-bound officer overwhelmed by personal loyalty and emotional strain

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A seemingly happy ending is undercut as Mr Helpmann and Jack appear, shaking their heads, revealing someone has escaped, followed by a cut to their point of view.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: appears with Mr. Helpmann in a surreal epilogue sequence, indicating oversight of a failed capture
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: present as a spectral authority figure observing an escape from beyond death
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: nature of reality in the final sequence
evidence_quotes: in the foreground, TWO HUGE HEADS appear looking straight at the camera. It is MR HELPMANN and JACK.
They both shake their heads.
MR HELPMANN He's got away from us, Jack.

status_updates: appears as a surviving authority figure in a dreamlike revision
auxiliary.persona_anchor: spectral guardian of bureaucratic order

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: SAM's IR room floats into a glorious sky as the melody of 'Brazil' plays, evoking nostalgia and a promise to return, ending the scene.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: final confirmation of Sam's mental escape
timeline_nodes.salient_development: acknowledges that Sam is mentally disengaged and unreachable
timeline_nodes.goal_state: acknowledge Sam's psychological departure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: accepts that Sam is no longer responsive to institutional control
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: reality of Sam's escape
evidence_quotes: JACK I'm afraid you're right, Mr. Helpmann. He's gone.
His eyes are open but miles away.
He is humming.
The Information Retrieval room with SAM in it floats away into the most beautiful glorious sky ever.

relation_updates: final acknowledgment of Sam's disengagement
auxiliary.persona_anchor: last voice of a failing system

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Jack begins as a symbol of bureaucratic success and loyalty to the system, contrasting with Sam's instability. Over time, he shifts from a supportive ally to an enforcer of institutional order, torn between personal ties and systemic duty. His emotional breakdown during Sam's interrogation reveals the cost of this conflict, culminating in his death. In the ambiguous epilogue, Jack reappears as a spectral authority, underscoring the persistence of systemic control even beyond death.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jill Layton bathes while watching TV in a bare flat; her view is blocked by a man in uniform, startling her as she asks who's there.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill is startled by a man in uniform who appears unexpectedly in her bathroom, obstructing her view of the TV while she bathes.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jill becomes alert and fearful in response to the uninvited presence in her private space.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: identity and intent of the man in uniform remain unknown
evidence_quotes: JILL LAYTON, washing the grime off herself while she watches Bilko in the mirror.
the T.V. screen is suddenly obscured by part of the body of a MAN in uniform.
JILL (scared) Who's there?

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as isolated and surveilled citizen

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jill and workmen appear through a ceiling hole, prompting security troops to aim guns; they retreat after Jill checks on Mrs. Buttle, who remains distressed over receipt books.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: establishing moral concern and active empathy
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill witnesses the aftermath of a violent raid and directly checks on Mrs. Buttle, showing compassion amid systemic brutality.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to confirm Mrs. Buttle's well-being
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jill is recognized as a concerned observer of the raid's aftermath, reacting with visible shock and initiating contact before retreating under threat.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the fate of Mr. Buttle and the safety of the Buttle family
evidence_quotes: JILL's shocked face appears looking down through the hole in the ceiling.
JILL Mrs Buttle, are you alright?
All three faces retreat.

relation_updates: demonstrates care for Mrs. Buttle
status_updates: emerges as a concerned observer of injustice
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as compassionate witness

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jill seeks help for a wrongful arrest but is redirected between departments; her unstamped receipt prevents progress, and a surveillance robot crashes after she swats it away.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: first active appearance showing agency and resistance
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill attempts to report a wrongful arrest but is blocked by bureaucratic procedures and targeted by intrusive surveillance.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to report a wrongful arrest and obtain proper documentation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jill is denied assistance due to an unstamped receipt and redirected back to a previous department, leaving her request unresolved
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: her attempt to correct a bureaucratic error is stalled by circular departmental protocols
evidence_quotes: JILL I want to report a wrongful arrest.
PORTER (looking at form) You want Information Adjustments. Different department.
PORTER Have you got an Arrest Receipt? JILL Yes. PORTER Is it stamped?
PORTER (examining receipt) No, there's no stamp on it. You see! I can't give you the form until it's stamped.

relation_updates: adversarial relationship with Information Porter
defiant stance toward surveillance technology
status_updates: blocked from accessing bureaucratic remedies due to unstamped receipt
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as bureaucratic resistor

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: SAM enters JILL's derelict flat, sees a hole in the floor, his car on fire outside, and JILL fleeing across the forecourt with a suitcase and bundles.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jill's flight from danger and Sam's failed reunion attempt
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill is observed leaving her flat with personal belongings, actively departing under urgent circumstances as Sam arrives.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Sam realizes Jill is in danger and must be pursued urgently.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sam transitions from passive observer to active pursuer in a high-stakes chase.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jill does not acknowledge Sam or allow contact.
evidence_quotes: JILL is apparently retreating from it across the forecourt.
She is carrying a suitcase and bundles.

relation_updates: Jill's relationship with Sam shifts from imagined to real and urgent
status_updates: Jill is now a fugitive with visible means of escape (suitcase, lorry)
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as fugitive agent

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jill threatens Porter with a bomb in her chest, claiming it's twenty pounds of high explosive, and demands he stamp her form, while verbally insulting him.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill threatens the porter by claiming her chest contains twenty pounds of high explosive, demanding he stamp her form, and warns she will blow the place up if he refuses.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to compel the porter to stamp her form through intimidation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: transforms the bureaucratic dispute into a perceived security threat
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the guards have not yet responded
evidence_quotes: You're a stupid, fat arsed, obstructive, fascist moron aren't you?
You think these are tits don't you?
I bet you'd like to touch them?
You're looking at twenty pounds of high explosive! And if you don't stamp this form I'm going to blow the place up!

relation_updates: hostile escalation toward porter
status_updates: claims possession of twenty pounds of high explosive
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as defiant provocateur

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam, panicked, reveals he's an Information Retrieval Officer DZ/015 and arrests Jill for her own good, urging her to flee; she starts driving coolly, telling him not to litter her cab.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill refuses to drive until Sam identifies himself and explains his authority, maintaining calm defiance despite his urgency.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to verify Sam's identity and intentions before taking action
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: in control of the vehicle and the decision to leave, on her own terms
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Sam is trustworthy or part of the oppressive system
evidence_quotes: JILL has switched off the engine and is sitting glaring at him.
JILL Who are you?
JILL (hard, icy cool) No.
JILL starts up and moves off, very cool, in her own time.

relation_updates: shifts from resistance to conditional cooperation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as calm and autonomous decision-maker

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam observes Jill exchanging parcels with a shadowy figure near a power plant, a scene mirroring a Ministry warning poster about suspicious activity, as a house is lowered onto a lorry.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill exchanges parcels with a shadowy figure near the power plant, an act observed by Sam and visually echoed by a Ministry warning poster.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: completing a covert transfer
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: parcels exchanged; activity witnessed by Sam and paralleled by Ministry poster
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: risk of exposure due to Sam's observation and the presence of a Ministry poster depicting similar behavior
evidence_quotes: JILL has made her way over to a rather SHADOWY FIGURE lurking around the corner of one of the metal structures.
They appear to be exchanging parcels.

relation_updates: engages in illicit coordination with unknown operative
acts in defiance of Ministry surveillance
status_updates: participates in black-market or resistance network
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as resistance operative

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam confronts a guard who transforms into a giant warrior; after being knocked down, Sam falls into a stone ship where prisoners morph into Baggees as he searches for Jill.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: witness and victim of state violence
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill reveals the parcel contains only executive toys meant as bribes, then shifts her attention to aiding the bombing victims before being struck by a security guard while trying to protect one.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: assist the injured and protect the vulnerable
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jill is physically assaulted by a security guard while helping the wounded, marking her active resistance to authoritarian force.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jill is detained or at risk of detention after being struck by the guard.
evidence_quotes: JILL There's your bomb! Our annual bribes for official ass-holes like you!
JILL's attention is attracted by moans coming from the badly hurt BOMB VICTIMS.
JILL Hey stop ... she's hurt! The GUARD gives JILL a thump in the side of the face with his gloved hand.

relation_updates: moral opposition to Security Guards
catalyst for Sam's protective rage
status_updates: publicly exposes the parcel as non-threatening
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as moral resistor

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam and Jill reunite, briefly kiss to avoid police suspicion, then flee together as Sam expresses concern for her safety.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill reunites with Sam in a high-risk environment, demonstrates awareness of surveillance, and immediately resumes a close partnership with him to evade detection.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: evade detection and stay together
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jill is now actively collaborating with Sam to avoid capture
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ongoing threat from surveillance state
evidence_quotes: a familiar voice comes from behind him. JILL You're late.
Stepping out from the shadows is JILL - cigarette in her mouth.
JILL (through kiss) They're gone.
They head off clutching one another.

relation_updates: reconnection with Sam Lowry
shift from separation to partnership
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as resilient partner

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam, stunned, tells Jill she's dead; she reads a print-out, jokes about necrophilia, and they rush to each other.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill learns from a print-out that she has been officially declared dead, reacts with dark humor, and embraces Sam, affirming their connection.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintaining connection and identity in the face of systemic erasure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jill acknowledges her legal nonexistence but prioritizes her emotional bond with Sam.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Her lack of legal identity creates ongoing vulnerability.
evidence_quotes: Jill Layton is dead.
She reads it and slowly looks up.
JILL Care for a bit of necrophilia?
They rush together.

relation_updates: mutual reaffirmation of love
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as defiantly human in the face of dehumanizing systems

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: JILL's truck, carrying a house, is driving dangerously through the streets and lurches around a corner.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: active agent evading capture and transporting Sam
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill drives her truck through the city with the house containing Sam, navigating sharply around corners under hazardous conditions.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: evading authorities while rescuing Sam
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: actively leading the escape with Sam in tow
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: safety of the escape route and destination
evidence_quotes: JILL's truck, with the house on its back, is driving dangerously through he streets.
It lurches round the corner.

relation_updates: assumes role of rescuer in relation to Sam
status_updates: in control of vehicle and mission
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as rescuer and driver of escape

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A truck with a house on its back is hidden in a valley, overgrown with creepers and roses, featuring a garden, animals, and a makeshift chimney emitting smoke; Jill appears collecting eggs.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Jill is seen living in a concealed truck-house in a valley, performing daily domestic tasks such as collecting eggs.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Jill is living off-grid in a hidden, self-maintained environment with signs of sustainable living, including a garden, livestock, and shelter.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: safety and permanence of the hidden refuge
evidence_quotes: Half hidden in the heart of the valley is the truck with the house on its back.
Creepers and wild roses have grown up over the truck and some of the house.
A curl of smoke rises from a makeshift chimney which has been attached to one of the walls.
A small piece of ground around the truck has been cleared and made into a pretty garden with a vegetable plat.

status_updates: residing in a concealed truck-house in a valley
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Jill as self-sufficient survivor

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Jill Layton evolves from a vulnerable individual under surveillance to an active resistor of bureaucratic oppression, driven by moral conviction and personal agency. Her journey traces a path from isolated citizen to fugitive operative, then to rescuer and partner in an off-grid sanctuary. She consistently challenges systemic absurdity, resists control, and asserts autonomy, ultimately embodying both resistance and intimate connection. Her relationship with Sam shifts from symbolic ideal to adversarial tension, then to mutual trust and domestic intimacy.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam defeats the horde, moves toward the cage to rescue the girl, but a dark, laughing cloud of black shapes rises and flies away; he hesitates to pursue when a ringing phone confuses him.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sam confronts the Forces of Darkness in a dream to rescue the girl but is distracted by a ringing phone before completing the rescue.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: rescue the girl from captivity
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sam has defeated the Forces of Darkness and approaches the cage but hesitates when a ringing phone disrupts the dream.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the source and meaning of the ringing phone in the dream
evidence_quotes: CUT TO SAM as he dives out of shot.
There at the end of the passage between two stone skyscrapers stands SAM ... barring the way.
But then the dreamy quality of this scene is interrupted by threatening rumble. SAM looks down.

relation_updates: Sam vs. Forces of Darkness (hostile)
Sam and the girl (idealized romantic connection)
status_updates: Sam gains control in dream combat
Sam hesitates due to external interruption
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as romantic idealist trapped in a mechanistic world

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jill seeks help for a wrongful arrest but is redirected between departments; her unstamped receipt prevents progress, and a surveillance robot crashes after she swats it away.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sam sees Jill's face on a surveillance screen and momentarily believes she is the girl from his dream, creating a psychological link between fantasy and reality.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sam is briefly startled by the image on the screen but dismisses the resemblance as imagined, returning to his routine
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the girl from the dream and Jill are connected remains uncertain
evidence_quotes: SAM happens to glance up at this moment. He is startled - the over-exposed TV image is the face of the GIRL FROM THE DREAM.
SAM decides he must have imagined it.
JACK What's the matter? SAM Sorry. Nothing. (snapping out of it) See you - I'm going to be late.

relation_updates: Sam becomes indirectly aware of Jill Layton
status_updates: Sam experiences a jarring moment of dream-reality confusion
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as disoriented observer caught between worlds

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: SAM rejects his promotion to Information Retrieval, throws the notice, and declares he wants nothing, not even dreams, as chaos ensues and a screen is set up around the table.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sam reacts with anger and rejection upon learning of his promotion to Information Retrieval, which he believes was arranged by his mother.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Sam resists professional advancement imposed by familial and institutional forces
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sam is officially transferred to Information Retrieval despite publicly rejecting the promotion
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Sam's desire for autonomy conflicts with his mother's influence and institutional control
evidence_quotes: MOTHER Actually, Alma, that's one of the little things I was dying to tell you ... Sam's been promoted to Information Retrieval.
SAM (angry and surprised) Mother!
SAM Stop this! (leaping to his feet) I'm not being promoted. I'm not going to Information Retrieval!
I don't want promotion. I don't want anything.

relation_updates: Sam's relationship with his mother becomes antagonistic
status_updates: Sam is officially transferred to Information Retrieval, Security Level 3
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as reluctant participant in a system he despises

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam, dazed, realizes the vision is gone; seeing only the empty ceiling hole, he rushes out as Mrs. Buttle shouts, desperate to find Jill who vanished.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sam sees Jill's reflection in a broken mirror, reacts with recognition and urgency, then rushes out to find her after she vanishes, shifting from disorientation to action.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: find Jill and understand her presence
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: dazed but driven to act
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jill's sudden disappearance and mysterious appearance
evidence_quotes: SAM (mumbles): It's you ... it's you ...
He looks for the vision on the floor but can't find it.
SAM rushes out of the flat.

relation_updates: recognizes Jill as real and significant
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as seeker crossing into personal agency

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam considers accepting a promotion to pursue Jill's dossier; Kurtzman reveals he accepted it for him. They leave but the lights go out, halting their exit.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sam attempts to access Jill Layton's classified dossier and considers accepting a promotion to gain higher clearance, but learns Kurtzman has already submitted the promotion paperwork on his behalf without his explicit consent.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to gain access to Jill Layton's classified information
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sam is bound to a bureaucratic advancement he did not formally accept, complicating his ability to act independently within the system.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to proceed with his investigation given that his career move was made without his full consent
evidence_quotes: SAM (preoccupied) A \"personnel\" transporter? They've got it wrong. I had a personal transporter. I'll do the paperwork tomorrow -
SAM You don't happen to know how I can get around an IRQ/3 do you?
KURTZMAN I did it for you.
SAM What! Shit!

relation_updates: Kurtzman acts on his behalf without consent
conflicted trust in bureaucratic system
status_updates: commits to career advancement for personal reasons
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as bureaucrat using the system against itself

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam flees from guards after abandoning his ID, running up stairs while being pursued by multiple guards following an incident in a restricted lift area.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sam fails to produce identification when challenged by a guard, abandons his attempt to comply, and flees up the stairs while being pursued.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to reach Jill before she leaves
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sam is actively being chased by multiple guards after evading identification check in a restricted area
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Sam is without accessible identification and is now evading security forces
evidence_quotes: SAM fumbles through his pockets desperate to get back to the lobby before JILL leaves.
SAM (frantic) Sorry, it'll have to wait. He runs off - dropping suit - towards some stairs he has spotted.
GUARD Stop!! Come back! He starts to run after SAM. TWO OTHER GUARDS also give chase.
INT. STAIRS DAY 75a SAM scrambles up the stairs. GUARDS in pursuit.

relation_updates: Sam becomes a target of security forces
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as fugitive breaking from institutional control

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam and Jill struggle for control of a lorry; Sam, a poor driver, fumbles with gear levers as security vehicles close in, prompting Jill's warning.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sam, unskilled in driving, fumbles with the lorry's gear levers while trying to evade security vehicles, escalating the danger.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Sam attempts to gain control or speed through trial and error
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sam's inexperience makes the lorry harder to control, endangering the escape
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Sam will cause a crash due to his lack of driving skill
evidence_quotes: SAM is terribly inexpert as a driver but he behaves like someone possessed.
He starts fumbling with he multiple gear levers.
JILL Don't touch those!
Through the rear view mirrors he can see the Security vehicles catching up with them.

relation_updates: Jill sees Sam as a direct threat to their survival
status_updates: security vehicles are visibly closing in
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as well-intentioned but ineffectual under pressure

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sam confronts a guard who transforms into a giant warrior; after being knocked down, Sam falls into a stone ship where prisoners morph into Baggees as he searches for Jill.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sam confronts Jill over the parcel, suspecting it contains a bomb, but his accusation is proven wrong when the parcel is never opened and an explosion occurs nearby—revealing his misjudgment and triggering shame.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: uncover truth and protect Jill
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: ashamed and disoriented after misjudging Jill and the situation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jill's true allegiance and motives
evidence_quotes: SAM grabs the parcel from the trolley and tries to start tearing it open.
JILL (grabbing parcel and trying to take it from him) No you're not!
They start an ugly little struggle for the parcel.
SAM's suit gets slightly ripped.

relation_updates: trust broken after physical struggle
emotional distance widens
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as flawed protector confronting his own delusions

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Security troops raid, seize Sam despite his protests, call him traitor, bag his head; a shot rings out as Jill screams while he's dragged away.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Security Troops raid the bedroom, seize Sam, and drag him away despite his protests, marking his arrest and separation from Jill.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: remain free with Jill and build a new life
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sam is in state custody, beginning formal processing
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Jill's safety and Sam's survival in the system are unknown
evidence_quotes: SAM (shouts) She's dead. Check the list! But it is SAM they have come for. He is being dragged out of the bed.
GUARDS (as they struggle with him) You Turncoat Bastard! JUDAS!! TRAITOR!!
A canvas bag is plunged over his head. All goes black.
SAM (muffled) JILL!!!A shot rings out. JILL screams. It echoes through the blackness.

relation_updates: Sam and Jill are forcibly separated
Security Troops establish violent control over Sam
status_updates: Sam is captured and labeled a traitor
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as victim of total institutional retribution

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: SAM learns Jill is dead, reacts with relief and guilt, then becomes catatonic as guards place a restraining bag over his head while HELPMANN departs with hollow platitudes.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sam learns Jill has been killed, briefly shows relief before registering guilt, then becomes unresponsive as authorities prepare him for further interrogation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: locate Jill and escape with her
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: non-responsive, immobilized with a restraining bag over his head
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the source of Sam's initial relief at hearing of Jill's death
evidence_quotes: SAM responds in a hoarse, urgent whisper, demanding to know where Jill is and what has happened to her.
SAM, after a moment, whispers, Dead? HELPMANN nods, explaining she was killed resisting arrest.
To his surprise, SAM appears relieved, muttering, No, no... I did that... He immediately stops, realizing his mistake.
SAM becomes catatonic, unresponsive.

relation_updates: Jill's death severs his last connection to hope and reality
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as a detainee who has ceased resisting

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: SAM's IR room floats into a glorious sky as the melody of 'Brazil' plays, evoking nostalgia and a promise to return, ending the scene.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: detached prisoner in a state of mental withdrawal
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sam remains physically restrained but mentally disengaged, showing signs of complete psychological retreat as the environment dissolves into a fantastical vision.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to return to a romanticized past with Jill
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically immobilized and declared mentally unresponsive, with no indication of reconnection to reality
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Sam's mental state reflects a sustained escape or a final break from reality
evidence_quotes: Sam is sitting in the IR chair. He is strapped in. His eyes are open but miles away. His face is wreathed in a benign and very happy smile.
He is humming.
The Information Retrieval room with SAM in it floats away into the most beautiful glorious sky ever.
I'm afraid you're right, Mr. Helpmann. He's gone.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sam as a figure of escapism under oppressive control

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Sam Lowry begins as a passive, bureaucratic functionary haunted by recurring dreams of rescuing a mysterious girl. His encounter with Jill Layton blurs fantasy and reality, driving him to defy the oppressive system in pursuit of connection and truth. As he descends into rebellion, his efforts to save Jill are thwarted by institutional violence, culminating in his capture and psychological collapse. Ultimately, Sam retreats into an irreversible fantasy, abandoning reality entirely.
